intro: Providence (from Latin providentia, foresight) denotes God's ongoing sovereign governance of all creation — his preserving all things in being and directing all events toward his purposes. Scripture presents God as actively sustaining the universe (Col. 1:17; Heb. 1:3), giving rain and seasons (Matt. 5:45; Acts 14:17), caring for birds and flowers (Matt. 6:26–30), numbering the hairs of each person's head (Matt. 10:30), and working all things together for good for those who love him (Rom. 8:28). The classic Old Testament illustration is Joseph's life: what his brothers meant for evil God meant for good (Gen. 50:20). Providence operates through both ordinary means (second causes) and miraculous intervention, and God's hidden governance does not diminish genuine human responsibility.
